{"step_by_step_thinking": "The fate of intermediate density lipoprotein (IDL) in plasma is described in the retrieved documents. Document [4] states: 'Approximately half of the newly formed IDL particles are taken up by hepatocytes through LDLR-ApoE-mediated endocytosis.' This directly supports option A: 50% is taken up by the liver. Document [5] and [3] indicate that 'the remaining half of the IDL loses its Apo-E and transforms into LDL through the action of hepatic lipase.' This confirms that 50% of IDL is converted to LDL, supporting option B. Since both A and B are accurate based on the evidence, the correct choice is C: Both 1 and 2.", "answer_choice": "C"}
